decks.notes: The deck focuses on utilizing Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to create a powerful combo that can quickly overwhelm opponents. It includes cards like Fleeting Spirit and Monument to Endurance to support the combo and protect key pieces. Thoughtseize and Fatal Push are included for disruption and removal, while cards like Iron-Shield Elf and Vanishing Verse help to control the board. The deck aims to establish a dominant board presence and quickly close out games using its combo potential.

The deck focuses on utilizing efficient creatures and disruptive spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive plays. By deploying mana dorks like Elvish Mystic and Llanowar Elves, the deck can ramp into powerful threats like Archon of Emeria and Skyclave Apparition. Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Aven Interrupter help to slow down the opponent's game plan, while Collected Company provides card advantage and allows for explosive turns. Overall, the deck aims to maintain board presence and pressure the opponent, forcing them to react and disrupting their strategy.

The deck revolves around assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo to generate value and overwhelm opponents. The strategy involves using cards like Bitter Triumph, Cache Grab, and Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent while setting up the combo pieces like Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II. Additional support is provided by cards like Esika's Chariot and Witherbloom Command for added versatility and resilience. Overall, the deck aims to outvalue opponents through synergistic interactions and efficient gameplay.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells. The key strategy is to play low-cost creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage alongside efficient burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage to quickly chip away at the opponent's life total. The deck also includes cards like Boomerang Basics and Heroes' Hangout to disrupt the opponent's game plan and maintain tempo. Overall, the deck aims to maintain a fast-paced aggressive strategy to pressure the opponent and secure victory.

The main focus of this Izzet Control deck is to maintain card advantage and control the game through a combination of counter spells, bounce effects, and removal spells. By utilizing card draw spells like Consider and Accumulate Wisdom, as well as creatures like Gran-Gran to generate card advantage, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while building towards its own win condition. With a mix of card advantage, disruption, and tempo plays, the deck seeks to outlast and outmaneuver its opponent in order to secure victory.

The main concept of this Orzhov Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan through hand disruption spells like Duress and Thoughtseize, removal spells like Fatal Push and Vanishing Verse, and powerful creatures like Gifted Aetherborn and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse. The deck aims to control the board state and outvalue the opponent with card advantage, backed up by versatile lands like Mutavault and Bleachbone Verge. The inclusion of Pest Control as a sideboard card suggests additional disruption against specific strategies, further solidifying the control aspect of the deck.

The main focus of this red aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with fast, aggressive creatures and burn spells. Using cards like Monastery Swiftspear, Soul-Scar Mage, and Emberheart Challenger to deal consistent damage, while also utilizing cards like Reckless Rage and Lightning Strike to remove any threats the opponent may have. With a mix of creatures and burn spells, this deck aims to quickly lower the opponent's life total and maintain control of the board to secure a quick victory.

The main focus of this deck is to put pressure on the opponent with efficient creatures and disruptive spells. By playing aggressive creatures like Archon of Emeria and Skyclave Apparition, along with cards like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben, the deck aims to control the board and keep the opponent on the back foot. Collected Company allows for explosive plays and card advantage, while Elspeth, Storm Slayer provides a powerful finisher. The mana base is carefully crafted to ensure consistent casting of spells and creatures, allowing the deck to maintain a relentless assault on the opponent.

The main idea of this deck is to control and disrupt your opponent's strategy while establishing your own powerful board presence. Utilizing a mix of removal spells, hand disruption, and efficient creatures, you aim to grind out your opponent and overwhelm them with resilient threats like Sentinel of the Nameless City and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse. The deck focuses on resource denial and flexibility, with cards like Abrupt Decay and Fatal Push providing answers to a wide range of threats. Overall, it seeks to outlast and outvalue your opponent in a slow, methodical manner.

This deck focuses on controlling the game through removal spells like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize while also utilizing powerful creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to dominate the battlefield. The deck also includes hand disruption with Duress and offers flexibility with cards like Tear Asunder and Abrupt Decay. The mana base is designed to support the deck's game plan, with cards like Llanowar Wastes and Mutavault providing the necessary mana sources. The overall strategy is to maintain board control and outvalue the opponent through efficient removal and powerful threats.

This deck focuses on using a combination of removal spells, hand disruption, and powerful creatures to control the game and outvalue the opponent. By utilizing cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to disrupt the opponent's game plan, as well as efficient threats like Badgermole Cub and Sentinel of the Nameless City to apply pressure, the deck aims to grind out the opponent and win through attrition. With a mix of removal, hand disruption, and threats, this deck looks to outmaneuver the opponent and come out ahead in the late game.

The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of cheap, aggressive creatures and burn spells to control the board and deal direct damage. By utilizing efficient creatures like Soul-Scar Mage and Monastery Swiftspear, along with removal spells like Reckless Rage and Torch the Tower, the deck can quickly whittle down the opponent's life total. The inclusion of counterspells like Academic Dispute and disruptive spells like Boomerang Basics help to further disrupt the opponent's game plan, allowing the deck to maintain pressure and secure a victory.

The main focus of this deck is to aggressively attack the opponent with low-cost creatures and burn spells, aiming to quickly reduce the opponent's life total before they have a chance to establish their own board presence. Creature abilities and direct damage spells are used to maintain pressure on the opponent, with the goal of winning the game through swift and relentless attacks. The deck is designed to be fast-paced and focused on overwhelming the opponent with a combination of efficient creatures and direct damage spells.

This Selesnya Aggro deck focuses on utilizing low-cost creatures to quickly overwhelm the opponent, with the added disruption of cards like Archon of Emeria and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben to slow down the opponent's game plan. The deck also includes cards like Collected Company for additional creature support and Skyclave Apparition for removal. The mana base is designed to consistently ramp up with Llanowar Elves and Elvish Mystic, allowing for efficient plays and powerful swings. Overall, this deck aims to establish board presence early and maintain pressure throughout the game.

This Boros Aggro deck aims to overwhelm opponents with quick, aggressive creatures like Fervent Champion and Kellan, the Fae-Blooded, while equipping them with powerful artifacts like Colossus Hammer and Leyline Axe for maximum damage potential. With cards like Sigarda's Aid and Mirrex to help play and equip artifacts at instant speed, this deck seeks to quickly close out games before the opponent has a chance to set up their own strategy. The inclusion of cards like Eiganjo, Seat of the Empire and Shadowspear provides additional versatility and utility to adapt to different matchups.

This deck focuses on using Lotus Field in combination with cards like Scapeshift and Arboreal Grazer to quickly generate a large amount of mana and ramp into powerful spells. By utilizing the land advantage provided by Lotus Field, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with big plays and combo potential. The addition of cards like Aftermath Analyst and Lumra, Bellow of the Woods provide additional support and versatility to the strategy, allowing for different lines of play and ensuring the deck can adapt to various situations.
